commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=73df28cae477aec053ddf4a75d32168b318681dc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes all whitespace from `web_fetch` URLs before parsing/fetching and adds a mocked regression test for a space after `https://`.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +17. Total +18 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main trims only leading/trailing URL whitespace, then passes the internal-space URL to `new URL(params.url)`, and a Node probe shows the reported scheme-space input throws. I did not run the full tool because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The author needs to revise the PR and supply real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue a repair marker while the external proof gate remains unmet.

target.title: fix(web_fetch): strip whitespace from URLs to prevent new URL() parse errors
target.body:
## Summary

LLMs occasionally generate `web_fetch` URLs with accidental spaces (e.g. `"https:// docs.example.com/page"`) which cause `new URL()` to throw `Invalid URL: must be http or https`. This fix strips all whitespace from the URL before parsing, so such URLs are accepted and fetched correctly.

## Linked context

Closes #91651

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `web_fetch` tool rejects URLs with internal whitespace that LLMs may generate (e.g. space after `https://`).
- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node v22.22.3, latest upstream/main
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/web-fetch.cf-markdown.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix (copied live output):**

```
 ✓ web_fetch Cloudflare Markdown for Agents > sends Accept header preferring text/markdown
 ✓ web_fetch Cloudflare Markdown for Agents > uses cf-markdown extractor
 ✓ web_fetch Cloudflare Markdown for Agents > falls back to readability
 ✓ web_fetch Cloudflare Markdown for Agents > bypasses Firecrawl
 ✓ web_fetch Cloudflare Markdown for Agents > logs x-markdown-tokens
 ✓ web_fetch Cloudflare Markdown for Agents > converts markdown to text
 ✓ web_fetch Cloudflare Markdown for Agents > does not log x-markdown-tokens
 ✓ web_fetch Cloudflare Markdown for Agents > strips whitespace from URLs with internal spaces (#91651)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** URLs with embedded spaces like `"https:// docs.example.com/page"` are cleaned to `"https://docs.example.com/page"` before `new URL()` parsing and before the HTTP fetch.
- **What was not tested:** Live HTTP fetch with space-containing URLs (mock test verifies the fetch URL is cleaned correctly).
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** None — the fix is a deterministic string operation.

## Tests and validation

**Which commands did you run?**
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/web-fetch.cf-markdown.test.ts
# 8 passed (1 test file, including 1 new regression test)
```

**What regression coverage was added or updated?**
Added `"strips whitespace from URLs with internal spaces"` — fetches a URL with spaces, verifies the fetch call uses the cleaned URL.

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — URLs with spaces now succeed instead of throwing errors.
**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No
**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No
**What is the highest-risk area?** Could cause fetches to unexpected URLs if space removal creates a different valid URL. Mitigation: protocol check (http/https) and SSRF guard remain in place.
**How is that risk mitigated?** URL is still validated after cleaning.

## Current review state

**What is the next action?** Maintainer review.
**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?** None.

---
